entry.title: How Much?
entry.summary: "There is nothing free in this world, but the grace of…
entry.content:
"There is nothing free in this world, but the grace of God."

Today, I want to talk about cost.

But not in the way you might be thinking.

Because sometimes, the most expensive things in our lives are free.

That is, they cost us the most.

The free things.

Now, some free things are cheap.

Some are priceless.

And some are worse than any loan you could ever sign.

In the business world, they talk about opportunity cost.

It's a fancy way of saying "Is this the best use of X? What do we lose out on by putting it here?"

In this case, X is money.

But X can be anything.

For if you put money here, you can't put it there.

Thus, there is lost.

That's the opportunity cost. You can't put it there, because you put it here.

It's a fine model.

One you should apply to your entire life, not just finances.

At what cost?

Is there a better place to put this (time, attention, energy)?

The answer is almost always a resounding "YES!"

You know what to do here.

And double down on all the times you say "No."

Do more of that.

They say if you have to ask how much it costs, you can't afford it.

But I say, like lots of things, they don't know what the fudge they talking about.

If you can afford it, always ask how much it costs.

You can't afford not to.

entry.title: Phatbergers
entry.summary: I got a confession to make. I want to help more…
entry.content:
I got a confession to make.

I want to help more people.

I really do.

But here's the thing:

I don't like people.

Most of them, anyway.

I'm sure I'm an assburger or whatever they call that blessing.

[Dramatic pause].

You seem alright, though.

I mean, you come here to read this sheet.

You're either crazy or cool.

Or both.

Either way, that's a good start.

I can work with that.

Just not now.

Give it time.

Until then, sorry folks, park's closed.

Sign outside should have told you.

But maybe, someday, I flip it over.

👐🍔

entry.title: Cyber and Space
entry.summary: So I'm dancing with ideas. 🎶 And we held the world,…
entry.content:
So I'm dancing with ideas.

🎶 And we held the world, we were turning it. 🎵

Like I do.

And it occurs to me that there are themes.

Genres.

In my life.

In my interests.

Iterating to Actualization.

Cyber and Space.

Quantum and Sacralization.

You ever wonder why they first called the internet and everything around it cyberspace?

It's this idea that there is so much in there (versus out).

A vastness.

With points of light in the mass of dark.

They say we analogize our brain with the latest technology.

So the brain is a computer, perhaps an ai one, and it is agentic to our soul.

And our universe is a cyberspace of sorts.

And if you really think about it, the only way to reach the real stars out there is to figure it all out inside these little boxes down here.

Cyber and Space.

💻🛸

To boldly go...[FARTS].

𖤂

entry.title: Fist on Chin
entry.summary: Welp. Today is/was a strange day. Sometimes, it be that way.
entry.content:
Welp.

Today is/was a strange day.

Sometimes, it be that way.

It occurs to me, that as little time as I put into this thing, I still need to have some focus.

And my mind is elsewhere.

Accumulating and processing data.

I never like reacting.

I'm pro active.

But this one, it's a thinker.

A best course of (re)actioner.

What's the correct response?

More important than a musings column.

At least for today.

entry.title: I Feel Better When I'm Dancing
entry.summary: When was the last time you cut a rug? I don't…
entry.content:
When was the last time you cut a rug?

I don't mean carpetry.

Is that a word?

I mean dancing.

When's the last time you got down?

Got low?

Got down to business?

When's the last time you danced?

Life is rhythm.

Life is frequency.

Life is motion.

Living rooms make great dance floors.

As do kitchens.

Cars.

Basically everywhere.

Heck, even the clubs. 

But I don't go to them anymore.

So I've been told by the people I love that I have terrible taste in music.

Like for instance, I don't like that iconic band from London named after an insect.

But I do like the radiation boys from Chicago.

What?

You got a problem with that?

You lucky I'm dancing.

🕺

entry.title: Good and Bad
entry.summary: Let's talk about good and bad. Not in an alignment sense,…
entry.content:
Let's talk about good and bad.

Not in an alignment sense, but sort of.

I mean quality. Ability. Competence.

These things are easy to see in professionals like doctors or lawyers.

A good lawyer can save your life.

A good doctor can stop you from being sewed.

Something like that.

But a bad one?

Woo boy.

The thing that I learned is that bad professionals can give great results.

A broken clock, after all.

And the other way.

Good ones can fail.

It's not a foolproof thing, because there are humans involved.

And here's the thing I wrestle with.

Would I rather have a bad lawyer or doctor who produced (through no ability of their own) a great result?

Or, a good one who sheet the proverbial bed?

I know the right way to think is the latter.

You want the latter.

The good on a bad day.

The good always.

But results driven people like me ponder.

Hesitate.

Great results are all that matter.

Right?

Right???

Perhaps I am too particular.

A poorly written cover letter just sours me.

As does a lack of charm. Or too much of it.

These are perhaps not the best way to gauge things, but alas, here we are.

Of course, a good professional delivers good results.

Consistently.

But it's not always so.

So I'll ask you, do you want the result or the intention?

The ends or the means?

You want a blind squirrel or Michelangelo on an off day?

entry.title: All My Life
entry.summary: Hello there. Today, I'd like to talk about love. When I…
entry.content:
Hello there.

Today, I'd like to talk about love.

When I was dying, or rather being poisoned to kill the thing trying to kill me before either killed me, I remember driving.

Up and down.

An hour each way.

Over and over.

To the poison centers that were trying to heal me.

And I remember, during one of these times, listening to a movie.

I didn't watch, just listened.

And it was about a fit Asian man.

Who loved to cook.

Who used embarrassing bravery to meet the girl of his dreams.

And say hello.

They fell in love.

And he got sick.

And, it didn't end well for him.

But she was so strong.

And I remember listening and thinking, "Is this how my story ends?"

And I remember, getting so angry.

So angry.

Fuq dat.

And it helped me. 

A lot this movie.

We are given 27,375 days.

Give or take.

If we are lucky.

I am lucky.

Either way.

🎵 "That first night we were standing at your door." 🎶

And how, in our home at least, and maybe yours too, every story is a love story.

And this is mine.

🎵 "Yeah, I see us in black and white

Crystal clear on a star lit night

In all your gorgeous colors

I promise that I'll love you for the rest of my life." 🎶

Sometimes, you don't want a Hollywood ending.

Because you never want it to end.

To my beautiful wife:

For giving me everything to ever fight for.

For being so strong.

For our story.

I promise.

🍌

entry.title: Gold Magic
entry.summary: If you were a song, what song would you be? I…
entry.content:
If you were a song, what song would you be?

I mean, what's your anthem?

Your theme song?

Your entrance song?

Your exit song?

"When it comes your time to die,

be not like those whose hearts are filled with the fear of death,

so that when their time comes they weep and pray

for a little more time to live their lives

over again in a different way.

Sing your death song

and die like a hero going home."

I mean, we got Sirius, from Alan Parsons.

Something by Tool.

Something by Pac.

Space Lord by Monster Magnet.

The clean version of the song's chorus goes "Space Lord, Mother, Mother."

Which I thought quirky and unique.

Only later did I realize that the original version had a crass word, and they used "Mother" twice instead of "Mother Bleeper."

But you know what?

I like Mother, Mother better.

Space Lord Mother Mother.

So I'd use the clean version.

My wife says the song's dirty.

Even clean.

And I suppose I am, too.

Dirty even clean.

🎵 "Take a sip from my secret potion,

I'll make you fall in love." 🎶

Hey!!!

We should have songs that define us.

Songs that just make us happy.

When we enter a room.

And when we leave it.

And everything in between.

🎵 "I could write it better than you ever felt it." 🎶

Hallelujah.

🎵 "I'm coming out the other side stronger.

One foot in front of the other.

Hands still raising.

Heart still praising." 🎶

Hallelujah, mother mothers.

entry.title: I Was Inverted
entry.summary: Mental models are an interesting thing. Basically, it's like a mini-rulebook…
entry.content:
Mental models are an interesting thing.

Basically, it's like a mini-rulebook on how to think and therefore how to act.

One of note is to invert.

Always invert.

Made famous by Charlie Munger (RIP), you basically start at the end result of what you do not want and work backwards.

Want a good life?

How do you guarantee that you will live a bad one?

Then, do everything you can to avoid the things you listed as living a bad life.

Poor Charlie used to always say, "Where will I pass?"

And then joke that he just would never go to that location, and thus, live forever.

It's an interesting way to think.

Want to be fit?

How do you get fat?

Want to be rich?

How do you stay poor?

Want to be successful?

How do you lose at life?

Sometimes, narrowing it down helps.

What to do based on what not to do.

Next time you're stuck, think about it from a different point of view.

Upside down.

Decide what you do not want.

And give it the finger.

✈️

🛩

entry.title: Late Again
entry.summary: A great actor playing a terrible role once told another actor…
entry.content:
A great actor playing a terrible role once told another actor on screen to "never go full absurd."

He said another word, but that's how I took it.

Never go full absurd.

That's the mistake.

Going maximal absurdity.

Leave a little room for normalcy.

Full absurd makes people squirmy.

Now, I typically enjoy that, but only in degrees.

And thus, believe it or not, I have to dial it down at times.

For the public.

For the column.

In pro wrestling, they say "just be yourself, dialed up to 11."

And in the modern day circus, this advice works.

But I'm just a dude in a spaceship, rocketing around the multiverse.

If I dialed it up to 11, I'd be in on Uranus.

🛸
